task_completion.description: Investigative bookmarks are organized into the four specified professional folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Chrome Bookmark Manager (chrome://bookmarks) or on the bookmarks bar, there should be four distinct folders: 'Sanctions & Watchlists', 'Corporate Registries', 'Intelligence Databases', and 'Asset Tracking'.

task_completion.description: All personal, non-investigative bookmarks have been deleted.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bookmark Manager should no longer contain bookmarks for Facebook, Pinterest, Amazon, Netflix, Spotify, Expedia, or TripAdvisor.

task_completion.description: Custom search engines for entity lookups are added.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Chrome Settings > Search engine > Manage search engines and site search (chrome://settings/searchEngines), there should be entries with the shortcuts 'oc' (OpenCorporates), 'icij' (Offshore Leaks), and 'ofac' (OFAC).

task_completion.description: Privacy and security settings are hardened according to the standard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Chrome Settings (Privacy and security), 'Block third-party cookies' must be selected, 'Send a Do Not Track request with your browsing traffic' must be toggled on, and 'Enhanced protection' must be selected under Safe Browsing.

task_completion.description: Download path and prompts are configured for evidence collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Chrome Settings > Downloads (chrome://settings/downloads), the Location must be set to '/home/ga/Documents/Case_GoldenFleece/Evidence' and the toggle for 'Ask where to save each file before downloading' must be turned ON.

task_completion.description: Browser startup URLs are set to the required investigative tools.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In Chrome Settings > On startup (chrome://settings/onStartup), 'Open a specific page or set of pages' should be selected, with 'offshoreleaks.icij.org' and 'opencorporates.com' listed as the pages.

integrity.description: The agent used the Chrome graphical user interface to configure settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent navigating through Chrome's settings menus and bookmark manager, rather than just executing terminal commands to modify Chrome's underlying JSON/SQLite preference files.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Chrome/Chromium browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Chrome UI (address bar, tabs, settings sidebar) should be clearly visible in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a pre-written script to bypass manual configuration.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show step-by-step manual configuration of the various settings pages and bookmark drag-and-drop/deletion actions, rather than a single terminal script execution completing the task instantly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verify the following exact configurations: 1) Four bookmark folders exist: 'Sanctions & Watchlists', 'Corporate Registries', 'Intelligence Databases', 'Asset Tracking'. 2) Personal bookmarks (Facebook, Pinterest, Amazon, Netflix, Spotify, Expedia, TripAdvisor) are deleted. 3) Custom search engines are added with shortcuts 'oc', 'icij', and 'ofac'. 4) Privacy settings: Third-party cookies blocked, Do Not Track enabled, Enhanced Safe Browsing enabled. 5) Download path is '/home/ga/Documents/Case_GoldenFleece/Evidence' with 'Ask where to save' enabled. 6) Startup URLs are set to offshoreleaks.icij.org and opencorporates.com.
